title: Coarse Menger Conjecture (Conjecture 1)
statement_text: For all $k,d\in\mathbb{N}$, there exists $c\in\mathbb{N}$ such that every graph $G$ and $X,Y\subseteq V(G)$ contains one of the following: $k$ $(X,Y)$-paths $P_{1},\dots,P_{k}$ such that $\operatorname{dist}_{G}(P_{i},P_{j})\geqslant d$ for all distinct $i,j\in[k]$; or a set $Z\subseteq V(G)$ with $|Z|\leqslant k-1$ such that $G-N_{G}[Z,cd]$ has no $(X,Y)$-path.
context_text: The Coarse Menger Conjecture was independently posed by Albrechtsen et al. and Georgakopoulos and Papasoglu as a central question in coarse graph theory, seeking a large-scale analogue of Menger's theorem where paths must be mutually far apart. It was subsequently disproved by Nguyen, Scott, and Seymour for all $d\geqslant 3$, and even for separators of size depending on a function of $k$. The paper studies the $\mathcal{A}$-path analogue (Conjecture 2) as a fruitful alternative.
attributed_to: Albrechtsen et al. and Georgakopoulos and Papasoglu
paper_contribution: The paper notes the conjecture has been fully disproved for $d\geq 3$ and pivots to the $\mathcal{A}$-path analogue (Geelen's Coarse Gallai Conjecture), resolving its $d=2$ case.

title: Geelen's Coarse Gallai Conjecture (Conjecture 2)
statement_text: There exist functions $f,g$ such that, for all $k,d\in\mathbb{N}$, every graph $G$ and vertex set $\mathcal{A}\subseteq V(G)$ contains one of the following: $k$ $\mathcal{A}$-paths $P_{1},\dots,P_{k}$ such that $\operatorname{dist}_{G}(P_{i},P_{j})\geqslant d$ for all distinct $i,j\in[k]$; or a set $Z\subseteq V(G)$ with $|Z|\leqslant f(k)$ such that $G-N_{G}[Z,g(d)]$ has no $\mathcal{A}$-path.
context_text: Motivated by the failure of the Coarse Menger Conjecture and the success of $\mathcal{A}$-path versions in group-labelled graph settings, Geelen posed this conjecture at the Barbados Graph Theory Workshop in March 2024. It is an $\mathcal{A}$-path analogue allowing the separator size and ball radius to depend on functions of $k$ and $d$, respectively. The paper resolves the $d=2$ case.
paper_contribution: Resolves the $d=2$ case by proving that every graph $G$ with $\mathcal{A}\subseteq V(G)$ contains $k$ pairwise anti-complete $\mathcal{A}$-paths or a set $Z$ with $|Z|\leqslant 78(k-1)$ such that $G-N_{G}[Z]$ has no $\mathcal{A}$-path (Theorem 3); also shows the general case reduces to the $d=3$ setting.

title: Conjecture 6
statement_text: For every $k\in\mathbb{N}$, every graph $G$ and $X,Y\subseteq V(G)$ satisfies one of the following: $G$ contains $k$ pairwise anti-complete $(X,Y)$-paths; or there exists a set $Z\subseteq V(G)$ with $|Z|\leqslant k-1$ such that $G-N_{G}[Z]$ has no $(X,Y)$-path.
context_text: The authors pose this $(X,Y)$-path strengthening of their main theorem (Theorem 3), seeking to reduce the separator size from $78(k-1)$ to the optimal $k-1$. If true, it would have immediate application to the study of induced minors, in particular implying an Induced Grid Minor Theorem for $K_{1,t}$-induced-subgraph-free graphs conjectured by Dallard et al.
